title: Alpine Relay: Convergence-Budget Saddle Descent
strategies: reference relay: plain gradient descent-ascent with the given conservative baseline step and no optimism (b=0); reproduces the evaluator's weak q_base and scores ~0.1

learning-rate tuning: keep the gains constant across all legs but grid-search the single best constant step via full-trajectory simulation; beats the untuned reference but is bottlenecked by the operator's extreme eigen-directions

designed schedule via per-leg residual minimization: exploit that the affine operator makes the next residual linear in (a,b) and solve the 2x2 normal equations over directions M g_t and M(g_t - g_{t-1}) each leg (Orthomin/Chebyshev-style 2D-Krylov acceleration), driving the operator norm orders of magnitude lower under the same budget

global schedule search / acceleration theory: optimize the whole degree-T residual polynomial (Chebyshev on the symmetrized spectrum, extragradient-with-momentum, or seeded coordinate search over all a[t],b[t]) to beat the myopic per-leg line search on ill-conditioned, strongly-coupled instances
